zh.Prescot, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾普雷斯可特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Holland於大約1469年出生在英國藍卡爾丹頓時，她的父親Richard Holland of Denton IV27歲，母親Isabella Harrington20歲。她於1490年與Robert Hedde在英國藍卡爾丹頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1502年3月8日在她的家鄉享年34歲。

zh-hans.Prescot, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾普雷斯可特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Holland于大约1469年出生在英国藍卡爾丹頓时，她的父亲Richard Holland of Denton IV27岁，母亲Isabella Harrington20岁。她于1490年与Robert Hedde在英国藍卡爾丹頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1502年3月8日在她的家乡享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howland
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Holland was born about 1469, in Denton, Lancashire, England, her father, Richard Holland of Denton IV, was 27 and her mother, Isabella Harrington, was 20. She married Robert Hedde in 1490, in Denton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 8 March 1502, in her hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Holland est née environ 1469, à Denton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Holland of Denton IV, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Isabella Harrington, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Hedde en 1490, à Denton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1502, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Holland um 1469 in Denton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Holland of Denton IV 27 und ihre Mutter Isabella Harrington 20. Sie hat 1490 in Denton, Lancashire, England Robert Hedde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. März 1502 im Alter von 34 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Holland è nata il circa 1469 nel Denton, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Richard Holland of Denton IV, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Isabella Harrington, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Robert Hedde nel 1490, nel Denton, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 8 marzo 1502, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Hollandが約1469年にイギリスランカシュアイアデントンで生まれた時，父Richard Holland of Denton IVは27歳，母Isabella Harringtonは20歳であった。1490年にイギリスランカシュアイアデントンでRobert Heddeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1502年3月8日に故郷で34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Holland(이)가 대략 1469년 영국 랭카실르 덴턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Holland of Denton IV(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Harrington(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 랭카실르 덴턴에서 Robert Hedde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1502년 3월 8일 그녀의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Holland nasceu aproximadamente 1469, em Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Holland of Denton IV, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Harrington, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Hedde em 1490, em Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1502, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Holland родилась около 1469 г. в местности дентон, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Holland of Denton IV было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Harrington, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Hedde в 1490 г. в местности дентон, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 8 марта 1502 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Holland nació aproximadamente 1469, en Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Holland of Denton IV, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Isabella Harrington, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Robert Hedde en 1490, en Denton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1502, en su pueblo, a la edad de 34 años.
